source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In Figures 6-1 and 6-2, the solid lines represent the open-loop logarithmic magnitude-frequency characteristics of two minimum-phase systems. The dashed portions in the figures indicate the changes in the open-loop logarithmic magnitude-frequency characteristics after applying series compensation. Which of the following statements correctly identifies the type of series compensation applied in Figures 6-1 and 6-2?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: In Figure 6-1, phase lag compensation was applied; in Figure 6-2, phase lead compensation was applied, with an increased cutoff frequency.

choice_map.A: In Figure 6-1, phase lead compensation was applied; in Figure 6-2, phase lag compensation was applied.
choice_map.B: In Figure 6-1, phase lag compensation was applied; in Figure 6-2, phase lead compensation was applied.
choice_map.C: In Figure 6-1, both phase lag and phase lead compensation were applied; in Figure 6-2, only phase lead compensation was applied.
choice_map.D: In Figure 6-1, phase lag compensation was applied; in Figure 6-2, phase lead compensation was applied, with an increased cutoff frequency.

agent_assessment.rationale: The question and answer choices are complete, and the task is clear: identify the compensation type from the two Bode magnitude plots. The image is necessary and broadly usable, but it is somewhat low-resolution/blurry, making the solid-versus-dashed curve distinction and fine slope labels a bit hard to verify confidently.
